ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tweet was created */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who posted the tweet */,
  "tweet_text" TEXT /* Original text of the tweet */,
  "tweet_punct" TEXT /* Tweet text with punctuation */,
  "tweet_tokenized" TEXT /* Tokenized version of the tweet text */,
  "tweet_nonstop" TEXT /* Tweet text with stop words removed */,
  "tweet_stemmed" TEXT /* Stemmed version of the tweet text */,
  "tweet_lemmatized" TEXT /* Lemmatized version of the tweet text */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign the tweet is associated with */,
  "engagement_score" INTEGER /* Overall engagement score of the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet has received */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies the tweet has received */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic the tweet is associated with */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the tweet (positive/negative/neutral) */,
  "tweet_tags" TEXT /* Tags associated with the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_topic_id FOREIGN KEY ("topic_id") REFERENCES topics ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the author (e.g., marketing analyst, social media manager) */,
  "twitter_handle" TEXT /* Twitter handle of the author */,
  "author_bio" TEXT /* Bio of the author */,
  "author_location" TEXT /* Location of the author */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the campaign */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the campaign (e.g., active, completed) */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet these metrics are associated with */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet has received */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies the tweet has received */,
  "impression_count" INTEGER /* Number of impressions the tweet has received */,
  "click_count" INTEGER /* Number of clicks the tweet has received */,
  "engagement_rate" REAL /* Engagement rate of the tweet (calculated as (retweets + likes + replies) / impressions) */,
  "video_views" INTEGER /* Number of video views for the tweet */,
  "video_clicks" INTEGER /* Number of video clicks for the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_metrics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., marketing analyst, social media manager, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "team" TEXT /* Team the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the tweet */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the tweet was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the topic */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the topic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_topics" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic associated with the tweet */,
  "relevance_score" REAL /* Relevance score of the topic to the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
